name: Lattice Coconut-Custard Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. In a large bowl, combine eggs, sugar, salt, vanilla, and milk; beat well.
3. Stir in shredded coconut. Pour mixture into the greased pie plate that has been set in a shallow baking pan.
4. Sprinkle with nutmeg. Pour enough hot water into the baking pan to come three-fourths up the side of the pie plate.
5. Bake 35 minutes, or until a silver knife inserted in the center comes out clean. Let cool at room temperature.
6. In a heatproof bowl, melt chocolate and shortening over hot (not boiling) water.
7. When the shell and custard are cool, loosen the custard from the sides of the pie plate using a small spatula.
8. Gently pull the custard away from the edges of the plate, tilting it until the custard is loose enough to slide out.
9. To form the chocolate topping, dip a metal or wooden skewer into the melted chocolate and draw it across the top of the pie in a crisscross pattern, dipping as needed.
10. Let set for a few minutes before serving.
